content: Just as 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems prioritize prioritizing high-uncertainty information to prune low-value stored content and improve long-term task performance, any general agent's developmental trajectory can be reframed as a dynamic active memory selection process where curiosity-driven novelty signaling prunes outdated low-impact beliefs to retain only high-uncertainty, high-gain knowledge. This reframing turns agent design from a problem of building fixed capability into tuning active memory pruning rules that match an agent's operating context.
